title: Engineering project managers and project engineers
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. (Based on 29 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Implement and coordinate mine safety programs, including the design and maintenance of protective and rescue equipment and safety devices.
Prepare schedules, reports, and estimates of the costs involved in developing and operating mines.
Write technical reports for engineering and management personnel, including those for mining, engineering, and other related fields.
Confer with scientific, engineering, and technical personnel to resolve design, research, and testing problems in mining operations.
Maintain records of drilling and production operations and monitor mine production rates to assess operational effectiveness.
Develop plans for oil and gas field drilling, product recovery, and treatment, while assessing costs and estimating production capabilities.
Assist engineering and operational personnel to solve problems, including directing completion and evaluation of wells or surveys.
Analyze data to recommend placement of wells and mechanisms to enhance oil and gas production, including well modification specifications.
Inspect mining areas and oil or gas wells for unsafe structures, equipment, and working conditions, ensuring compliance with safety standards.
Select locations and plan underground or surface mining operations to ensure safe and environmentally sound extraction of resources.
Evaluate findings to develop, design, or test mining equipment or processes, and conduct engineering research experiments related to mining operations.
Supervise, train, and evaluate technicians or engineers involved in mining operations, while coordinating project activities across teams.
Design, implement, and monitor mining and mineral treatment equipment and machinery in collaboration with other engineering specialists.
Conduct validation tests and audits of new or existing processes, equipment, or methods to ensure compliance with internal or external standards.
Prepare documentation, including reports and specifications, for production processes, equipment, or engineering procedures in mining.
Evaluate the economic viability of new projects by analyzing production costs, staffing requirements, and manufacturing processes.
Design and implement environmental controls in mining and oil operations to mitigate pollution and ensure compliance with regulations.
Communicate with management and staff to develop production and design standards based on engineering specifications and operational needs.
Integrate human factors requirements into operational hardware to optimize safety and efficiency in mining operations.
Perform acceptance testing on newly installed or updated systems.
Perform site analyses to determine system configurations, processes to be impacted, or obstacles to technology implementation.
Develop process flows, work instructions, or standard operating procedures for RFID systems.
Define and compare possible RFID solutions to inform selection for specific projects.
Determine means of integrating RFID into other applications and collect data about client hardware and software.
Create simulations or models of RFID systems and test RFID software to ensure proper functioning.
Confer with engineers, customers, vendors, or others to discuss electronics engineering projects or products.
Determine project material or equipment needs and evaluate project work to ensure technical adequacy.
Analyze system requirements, capacity, cost, or customer needs to determine project feasibility.
Provide technical support or instruction to staff or customers regarding electronics equipment standards.
Recommend repair or design modifications of electronic components based on service or cost factors.
Design electronic components or systems for various applications and inspect them to ensure conformity to standards.
Prepare documentation containing technical specifications or information about performance weaknesses.
Direct or coordinate activities related to manufacture, installation, or modification of electronic systems.
Develop operational, maintenance, or testing procedures for electronic products or systems.
Maintain databases of experiment characteristics or results and prepare technical reports or documents.
Conduct training to educate personnel on proper use of equipment and safety laws.
Analyze, interpret, or create graphical representations of energy data, using engineering software.
Promote awareness or use of alternative or renewable energy sources while monitoring energy consumption.
Manage teams of engineers and oversee project timelines, budgets, and contract obligations.
Conduct research to evaluate safety levels for products and assess health risks related to technology.
Evaluate product designs for safety and conduct testing to determine design functionalities.
Design nano-based manufacturing processes to minimize water, chemical, or energy use, as well as to reduce waste production.
Design robotics applications for manufacturers of green products, such as wind turbines or solar panels, to improve operations and reduce costs.
Identify and recommend energy savings strategies to achieve more energy-efficient operation.
Design or program robotics systems for environmental clean-up applications to minimize human exposure to hazards.
Design photonics products, such as light sources, displays, or photovoltaics, to achieve increased energy efficiency.
Conduct energy audits to evaluate energy use and identify conservation and cost reduction measures.
Confer with research and biomanufacturing personnel to ensure compatibility of design and production.
Communicate with suppliers regarding design or specifications of bioproduction equipment and materials.
Evaluate robotic systems or prototypes to ensure effectiveness and performance.
Build, configure, or test robots or robotic applications to meet operational requirements.
Monitor energy-related design or construction issues, such as energy management or sustainable design.
Research renewable or alternative energy systems or technologies, such as solar thermal or photovoltaic energy.
Prepare technical reports of findings or recommendations based on synthesized analyses of test results.
Provide consultation or training on topics such as mechatronics or automated control to enhance project outcomes.
Evaluate the safety, efficiency, and effectiveness of biomedical equipment based on design specifications.
Conduct research on fire retardants and the fire safety of materials and devices.
Design or develop nuclear equipment, such as reactor cores or radiation shielding, to enhance safety and performance.
Create models to optimize the layout of wind farm access roads or other infrastructure.
Conduct research related to nanotechnology topics such as nanocomposites, nanofabrication, or optoelectronics.
Design robotic systems for applications such as autonomous vehicles, advanced sensing, or automated production operations.
Develop models or computer simulations of biobehavioral systems to measure or control life processes.
Conduct analyses addressing issues such as failure, reliability, or yield improvement, and investigate mechanical failures or unexpected maintenance problems.
Develop formal documentation for microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) devices, including quality assurance guidance and control protocols.
Create schematics and physical layouts of integrated microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) components or packaged assemblies.
Conduct or supervise tests on raw materials or finished products to ensure their quality, along with monitoring material performance and evaluating deterioration.
Design and direct the testing or control of processing procedures and establish arrangement of functional equipment.
Calculate the efficiency or power output of fuel cell systems and analyze fuel cell test data using statistical software.
Plan and implement laboratory operations to develop material and fabrication procedures that meet cost and performance standards.
Design agricultural machinery components and equipment, and test agricultural machinery to ensure adequate performance.
Perform failure, variation, or root cause analyses and modify designs to obtain specified functional or operational performance.
Write performance requirements for product development or engineering projects and prepare technical reports related to engineering projects.
Conduct environmental, operational, or performance tests on marine machinery and equipment, and evaluate performance during dock and sea trials.
Evaluate product data or designs from inspections or reports for conformance to engineering principles and quality standards.
Discuss plans with clients, contractors, and other engineers for evaluation and necessary changes based on requirements.
Develop specifications for vehicles powered by alternative fuels or alternative power methods, and design vehicles for increased recyclability.
Supervise the work of technologists, technicians, and other engineers and scientists, and conduct training sessions on new materials or methods.
Prepare reports, sketches, drawings, specifications, and budgets for proposed sites or systems, while ensuring compliance to safety standards.
Research and analyze customer design proposals to evaluate feasibility, cost, or maintenance requirements of designs or applications.
Investigate equipment failures or difficulties to diagnose faulty operation and recommend remedial actions, along with providing technical direction to support personnel.
Coordinate production activities with other functional units, such as procurement and quality control, and oversee installation and repair of marine equipment.
Conduct research studies to develop new concepts in engineering, particularly in automotive and aerospace fields.
Recommend or implement changes to fuel cell system designs.
Characterize component or fuel cell performances by defining operating conditions or executing durability assessments.
Provide technical consultation or direction related to the development of fuel cell systems.
Design or implement fuel cell testing or development programs and prepare data acquisition systems for these tests.
Conduct post-service or failure analyses using electromechanical diagnostic principles.
Plan or implement fuel cell cost reduction or product improvement projects in collaboration with engineers and customers.
Coordinate fuel cell engineering schedules with departments outside engineering, such as manufacturing.
Authorize the release of fuel cell parts, components, or subsystems for production.
Manage fuel cell battery hybrid system architecture, including sizing components like fuel cells and energy storage units.
Conduct fuel cell testing projects using test stations, analytical instruments, or electrochemical diagnostics.
Define specifications for fuel cell materials and evaluate the environmental impact of fuel cell system designs.
Develop fuel cell materials, fabricate prototypes of components, and test new equipment.
Identify vehicle integration challenges for fuel cell vehicles and integrate electric drive subsystems to optimize performance.
Plan or conduct experiments to validate new materials and optimize processes for fuel cells.
Direct or coordinate activities of personnel involved in designing or testing of aerospace products.
